records.ety: [OE. wimpel, AS. winpel; akin to D. & G. wimpel a pennant, streamer, OHG. wimpal a veil, Icel. vimpill, Dan. & Sw. vimpel a pennant, streamer; of uncertain origin. Cf. Gimp.]

defs: A covering of silk, linen, or other material, for the neck and chin, formerly worn by women as an outdoor protection, and still retained in the dress of nuns.
quotes: Full seemly her wympel ipinched is.
For she had laid her mournful stole aside, And widowlike sad wimple thrown away.
Then Vivian rose, And from her brown-locked head the wimple throws.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Wimpled (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Wimpling (?).]

defs: To clothe with a wimple; to cover, as with a veil; hence, to hoodwink.
quotes: This wimpled, whining, purblind, wayward boy.

defs: To draw down, as a veil; to lay in folds or plaits, as a veil.
To cause to appear as if laid in folds or plaits; to cause to ripple or undulate; as, the wind wimples the surface of water.
To lie in folds; also, to appear as if laid in folds or plaits; to ripple; to undulate.
quotes: For with a veil, that wimpled everywhere, Her head and face was hid.
With me through . . . meadows stray, Where wimpling waters make their way.
